item.title: CVE-2026-46050 md/raid10: fix deadlock with check operation and nowait requests
CVE-2026-46011 media: mtk-jpeg: fix use-after-free in release path due to uncancelled work
CVE-2026-45877 HID: intel-ish-hid: fix NULL-ptr-deref in ishtp_bus_remove_all_clients
CVE-2026-45917 ipvs: do not keep dest_dst if dev is going down
CVE-2026-45841 netfilter: nfnetlink_osf: fix divide-by-zero in OSF_WSS_MODULO
CVE-2026-46062 ntfs3: fix integer overflow in run_unpack() volume boundary check
CVE-2026-46005 xfs: fix a resource leak in xfs_alloc_buftarg()
CVE-2026-45930 net: mctp: ensure our nlmsg responses are initialised
CVE-2026-46021 thermal: core: Fix thermal zone governor cleanup issues
CVE-2026-46037 ipv4: icmp: validate reply type before using icmp_pointers
CVE-2026-46084 RDMA/mana_ib: Disable RX steering on RSS QP destroy
CVE-2026-46012 rxrpc: Fix memory leaks in rxkad_verify_response()
CVE-2026-46085 rxrpc: Fix rxkad crypto unalignment handling
CVE-2026-46059 KVM: nSVM: Always use NextRIP as vmcb02's NextRIP after first L2 VMRUN
CVE-2026-46004 ALSA: caiaq: Handle probe errors properly
CVE-2026-45901 netfilter: nf_tables: revert commit_mutex usage in reset path
CVE-2026-46080 ocfs2: split transactions in dio completion to avoid credit exhaustion
CVE-2026-45894 iommu/vt-d: Clear Present bit before tearing down PASID entry
CVE-2026-45840 openvswitch: cap upcall PID array size and pre-size vport replies
CVE-2026-46054 selinux: fix overlayfs mmap() and mprotect() access checks
CVE-2026-45991 udf: fix partition descriptor append bookkeeping
CVE-2026-46027 net/smc: avoid early lgr access in smc_clc_wait_msg
CVE-2026-46088 ALSA: control: Validate buf_len before strnlen() in snd_ctl_elem_init_enum_names()
CVE-2026-46051 md/raid5: fix soft lockup in retry_aligned_read()
CVE-2026-46053 net: rds: fix MR cleanup on copy error
CVE-2026-46018 ALSA: usb-audio: stop parsing UAC2 rates at MAX_NR_RATES
CVE-2026-45835 Bluetooth: L2CAP: Fix null-ptr-deref in l2cap_sock_new_connection_cb()
CVE-2026-45834 Bluetooth: L2CAP: Fix null-ptr-deref in l2cap_sock_state_change_cb()
CVE-2026-45944 iommu/vt-d: Clear Present bit before tearing down context entry
CVE-2026-45932 bpf: Fix tcx/netkit detach permissions when prog fd isn't given
CVE-2026-45836 Bluetooth: L2CAP: Fix null-ptr-deref in l2cap_sock_get_sndtimeo_cb()
CVE-2026-45961 gfs2: fix memory leaks in gfs2_fill_super error path
CVE-2026-44844 eml_parser: Recursion DoS via nested message/rfc822 attachments
CVE-2026-45839 bpf: reject negative CO-RE accessor indices in bpf_core_parse_spec()
CVE-2026-45940 net: stmmac: fix oops when split header is enabled
CVE-2026-44708 Mistune Math Plugin XSS Escape Bypass
CVE-2026-44897 Mistune Heading ID Attribute Injection XSS
CVE-2026-45893 apparmor: Fix & Optimize table creation from possibly unaligned memory
CVE-2026-45943 erofs: fix inline data read failure for ztailpacking pclusters
CVE-2026-46017 mm: fix deferred split queue races during migration
CVE-2026-45897 netfilter: nft_counter: serialize reset with spinlock
CVE-2026-45997 scsi: sd: fix missing put_disk() when device_add(&disk_dev) fails
CVE-2026-45986 crypto: ccree - fix a memory leak in cc_mac_digest()
CVE-2026-47104 libusb < 1.0.30 Out-of-Bounds Read in parse_iad_array()
CVE-2026-46047 net: qrtr: ns: Fix use-after-free in driver remove()
CVE-2026-45571 go-git: Crafted repositories may modify main and submodule .git directories
CVE-2026-45850 ipvs: skip ipv6 extension headers for csum checks
CVE-2026-46052 ceph: only d_add() negative dentries when they are unhashed
CVE-2026-46009 PCI: endpoint: pci-epf-ntb: Remove duplicate resource teardown
CVE-2026-46070 md/raid5: validate payload size before accessing journal metadata
CVE-2026-46043 RDMA/rxe: Validate pad and ICRC before payload_size() in rxe_rcv
CVE-2026-45994 ibmasm: fix OOB reads in command_file_write due to missing size checks
CVE-2026-46069 wifi: mwifiex: fix use-after-free in mwifiex_adapter_cleanup()
CVE-2026-45859 netfilter: nfnetlink_queue: do shared-unconfirmed check before segmentation
CVE-2026-46032 KVM: nSVM: Triple fault if restore host CR3 fails on nested #VMEXIT
CVE-2026-46101 netfilter: reject zero shift in nft_bitwise
CVE-2026-46014 KVM: SVM: Add missing save/restore handling of LBR MSRs
CVE-2026-45845 net/sched: taprio: fix NULL pointer dereference in class dump
CVE-2026-46086 net: bridge: use a stable FDB dst snapshot in RCU readers
CVE-2026-46065 fbdev: defio: Disconnect deferred I/O from the lifetime of struct fb_info
CVE-2026-46098 net: caif: clear client service pointer on teardown
CVE-2026-45861 gfs2: Fix slab-use-after-free in qd_put
CVE-2026-46077 crypto: atmel-tdes - fix DMA sync direction
CVE-2026-46063 x86/shstk: Prevent deadlock during shstk sigreturn
CVE-2026-46056 Bluetooth: hci_event: fix potential UAF in SSP passkey handlers
CVE-2026-45956 drm/exynos: vidi: use priv->vidi_dev for ctx lookup in vidi_connection_ioctl()
CVE-2026-46068 crypto: nx - fix bounce buffer leaks in nx842_crypto_{alloc,free}_ctx
CVE-2026-45843 slip: bound decode() reads against the compressed packet length
CVE-2026-46024 libceph: Prevent potential null-ptr-deref in ceph_handle_auth_reply()
CVE-2026-45963 ASoC: nau8821: Cancel delayed work on component remove
CVE-2026-45998 rxrpc: Fix potential UAF after skb_unshare() failure
CVE-2026-46023 dm mirror: fix integer overflow in create_dirty_log()
CVE-2026-45844 netfilter: arp_tables: fix IEEE1394 ARP payload parsing
CVE-2026-45892 ext4: drop extent cache after doing PARTIAL_VALID1 zeroout
CVE-2026-46022 misc: ibmasm: fix OOB MMIO read in ibmasm_handle_mouse_interrupt()
CVE-2026-46102 net: strparser: fix skb_head leak in strp_abort_strp()
CVE-2026-46016 remoteproc: xlnx: Only access buffer information if IPI is buffered
CVE-2026-46000 rxrpc: Fix conn-level packet handling to unshare RESPONSE packets
CVE-2025-71305 drm/display/dp_mst: Add protection against 0 vcpi
CVE-2026-46006 drm/nouveau: fix u32 overflow in pushbuf reloc bounds check
CVE-2026-46003 net: qrtr: ns: Limit the total number of nodes
CVE-2026-46048 ALSA: caiaq: fix usb_dev refcount leak on probe failure
CVE-2026-46002 ext2: reject inodes with zero i_nlink and valid mode in ext2_iget()
CVE-2026-46078 erofs: fix the out-of-bounds nameoff handling for trailing dirents
CVE-2026-46064 ibmasm: fix heap over-read in ibmasm_send_i2o_message()
CVE-2026-46075 crypto: atmel-sha204a - Fix potential UAF and memory leak in remove path
CVE-2026-45973 RDMA/mlx5: Fix UMR hang in LAG error state unload
CVE-2026-45838 bpf: fix end-of-list detection in cgroup_storage_get_next_key()
CVE-2026-45899 ext4: drop extent cache when splitting extent fails
CVE-2026-46071 KVM: nSVM: Avoid clearing VMCB_LBR in vmcb12
CVE-2026-46049 ALSA: ctxfi: Add fallback to default RSR for S/PDIF
CVE-2026-46066 ceph: fix num_ops off-by-one when crypto allocation fails
CVE-2026-45989 of: unittest: fix use-after-free in testdrv_probe()
CVE-2026-45855 ata: libata-scsi: avoid Non-NCQ command starvation
CVE-2026-46058 media: amphion: Fix race between m2m job_abort and device_run
CVE-2026-46031 net: ks8851: Reinstate disabling of BHs around IRQ handler
CVE-2026-45912 ext4: don't cache extent during splitting extent
CVE-2026-45999 erofs: fix unsigned underflow in z_erofs_lz4_handle_overlap()
CVE-2026-44896 Mistune: XSS via unescaped figclass/figwidth in Figure directive
CVE-2026-46091 media: rc: igorplugusb: heed coherency rules
CVE-2026-45958 drm/exynos: vidi: fix to avoid directly dereferencing user pointer
CVE-2026-45846 bareudp: fix NULL pointer dereference in bareudp_fill_metadata_dst()
CVE-2026-44899 Mistune Image Directive CSS Injection Vulnerability
CVE-2026-44898 Mistune TOC Anchor Injection XSS
CVE-2026-46089 zram: do not forget to endio for partial discard requests
CVE-2026-46033 crypto: authencesn - reject short ahash digests during instance creation
CVE-2026-46046 ext4: fix missing brelse() in ext4_xattr_inode_dec_ref_all()
CVE-2026-23679 libusb < 1.0.30 NULL Pointer Dereference in parse_interface()
CVE-2026-45570 go-git: Improper single-quote escaping in go-git SSH transport
CVE-2026-46038 net: qrtr: ns: Free the node during ctrl_cmd_bye()
CVE-2026-46040 inotify: fix watch count leak when fsnotify_add_inode_mark_locked() fails
CVE-2026-45988 rxrpc: Fix re-decryption of RESPONSE packets
CVE-2026-45996 spi: imx: fix use-after-free on unbind
CVE-2026-45942 ext4: fix e4b bitmap inconsistency reports
CVE-2026-46019 crypto: atmel-aes - Fix 3-page memory leak in atmel_aes_buff_cleanup
CVE-2026-46103 can: ucan: fix devres lifetime
CVE-2026-46092 wifi: rtw88: check for PCI upstream bridge existence
CVE-2026-45842 slip: reject VJ receive packets on instances with no rstate array
CVE-2026-45949 hwrng: core - use RCU and work_struct to fix race condition
CVE-2026-46044 ipmi:ssif: Clean up kthread on errors
CVE-2026-46072 ntfs3: add buffer boundary checks to run_unpack()
CVE-2026-46079 rbd: fix null-ptr-deref when device_add_disk() fails
CVE-2026-46099 net: ipv6: fix NOREF dst use in seg6 and rpl lwtunnels
CVE-2026-46083 spi: fix resource leaks on device setup failure
CVE-2026-45987 KVM: nSVM: Sync interrupt shadow to cached vmcb12 after VMRUN of L2
CVE-2026-46015 tcp: call sk_data_ready() after listener migration
CVE-2026-45858 ext4: don't zero the entire extent if EXT4_EXT_DATA_PARTIAL_VALID1
CVE-2026-45934 btrfs: fix EEXIST abort due to non-consecutive gaps in chunk allocation
CVE-2026-46090 ALSA: aloop: Fix peer runtime UAF during format-change stop
CVE-2026-46082 KVM: SVM: Inject #UD for INVLPGA if EFER.SVME=0
CVE-2026-45993 LoongArch: Add spectre boundry for syscall dispatch table
CVE-2026-46026 net: qrtr: ns: Limit the maximum number of lookups
CVE-2026-46076 KVM: nSVM: Raise #UD if unhandled VMMCALL isn't intercepted by L1
CVE-2026-46094 ext4: fix bounds check in check_xattrs() to prevent out-of-bounds access
GlassWorm falls, but the repo problem is far from solved
item.description: Taking down a sprawling malware operation once signaled progress in securing the open-source ecosystem. Now, it barely registers. The GlassWorm campaign disruption comes at a moment when attackers can quickly reconstitute, and defenders are increasingly grappling with a new chall…

item.title: Authenticated RCE via Argument Injection in Gogs (NOT FIXED)
item.description: Overview Rapid7 Labs discovered a critical argument injection ( CWE-88 ) vulnerability in Gogs , a popular open-source self-hosted Git service. Rapid7 Labs scores this vulnerability as CVSSv4 9.4 (Critical). The vulnerability allows any authenticated user to achieve remote code e…

item.title: VU#780781: Casdoor contains multiple authentication bypass and access management vulnerabilities
item.description: Overview Casdoor versions 2.362.0 and earlier contain several identity and access management vulnerabilities that enable broad authentication bypass and privilege escalation. These flaws relate to Casdoor’s Security Assertion Markup Language (SAML) processing, account binding, an…

item.title: Employees are unknowingly inviting tech support impersonators into firms, says FBI
item.description: Online or telephone IT support scams have been tricking employees into downloading or clicking on malware for years. But according to the FBI, one group that targets US-based law firms has recently found success in person, by convincing firms to allow a supposed IT support person…

item.title: Graduation day grifts
The bipartisan case for CISA.
item.description: This week, Dave and Ben sit down to discuss a growing bipartisan effort to support CISA. Throughout the conversation, the two look at how lawmakers from both sides of the aisle are showing greater support for CISA after the Trump administration cut its budget and workforce. Both …

item.title: Companies built AI into core systems before figuring out how to govern it
item.description: 70% of organizations use GenAI in live environments, and 64% have AI agents in pilot or production deployments. Some of those agents have privileged access to core systems, according to Check Point’s 2026 Cloud Security Report. Confirmed and suspected AI incidents (Source: Check …

item.title: Canonical releases Workshop for one-command sandboxed dev environments on Ubuntu
item.description: Canonical released Workshop, a tool that launches sandboxed development environments on Ubuntu with a single command. Environments are configured once and reproduced on different machines, giving teams consistent setups across development workstations and deployment pipelines. A …

item.title: Hottest cybersecurity open-source tools of the month: May 2026
item.description: Presented here is a curated selection of noteworthy open-source cybersecurity solutions that have drawn recognition for their ability to enhance security postures across diverse settings. Pipelock: Open-source AI agent firewall AI coding agents run with shell access, environment …

item.title: Kemper - 269,299 breached accounts
item.description: In April 2026, the American insurance holding company Kemper Corporation was named by the ShinyHunters ransomware group in a "pay or leak" extortion campaign . The attackers allegedly accessed Kemper's Salesforce environment via social engineering as part of a broader campaign ta…

item.title: Ketch brings multi-agent AI orchestration to enterprise privacy programs
item.description: Ketch has unveiled its vision for agentic privacy with the Ketch Agent Network, a multi-agent orchestration layer for enterprise privacy programs. The platform is designed to continuously reason across legal obligations, internal policies, and operational realities within a unifi…

item.title: What the industrialization of exploitation means for defenders
item.description: For decades, cybersecurity was a battle of skill. Elite attackers versus elite defenders. The rules of engagement were understood, even if the playing field wasn’t level. If you hired better analysts and bought better tools, hopefully you hardened your systems well enough and bui…

item.title: Download pumping: New npm deception technique for supply chain attacks
item.description: Learn how attackers exploit automated bot traffic as part of software supply chain attacks to artificially inflate download counters and mask malicious payloads as legitimate. Key takeaways Volume doesn’t equal trust. Packages with numerous versions and high download counts might…

item.title: Microsoft’s new cloud PCs place AI agents under enterprise controls
item.description: Microsoft’s Windows 365 for Agents, a cloud PC platform for agentic workloads, runs AI agents in secure environments. Organizations can direct agents with natural language to interact with applications, browsers, files, and enterprise systems. The platform is available in public …

item.title: Oil shipments, drone makers, and a poisoned code library targeted in recent APT campaigns
item.description: Geopolitical pressure drove much of the state-sponsored cyber activity recorded between October 2025 and March 2026, according to ESET’s latest APT Activity Report. Espionage groups aligned with China, North Korea, Russia, and Iran adjusted their targets to match the econom…

item.title: The AI governance imperative you can’t afford to ignore
item.description: CIOs rushing to roll out AI agents without real visibility into their decision-making processes are flirting with disaster. According to AI experts, deploying agents without observability processes and tools creates a ticking time bomb with the potential for huge negative consequ…

item.title: DICOM, Pydicom, GDCM, and Orthanc: A technical tour of what really happens in the heap
item.description: This white paper presents a concrete case study demonstrating the creation of a heap overflow vulnerability through the exploitation of the DICOM file format.

item.title: Microsoft Condemns "Uncoordinated" Zero Day Disclosures
item.description: Microsoft warned the disclosure of several unpatched vulnerabilities without notice has put “customers at unnecessary risk”

item.title: Gitea Vulnerability Exposed 30,000 Deployments to Attacks
item.description: The security flaw allowed attackers to pull private container images, exposing source code, credentials, and infrastructure. The post Gitea Vulnerability Exposed 30,000 Deployments to Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Critical FortiClient EMS Vulnerability Exploited in Fresh Attacks
item.description: Fortinet rolled out hotfixes for the security defect in April, warning that it had been exploited in the wild as a zero-day and urging immediate patching. The post Critical FortiClient EMS Vulnerability Exploited in Fresh Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: IBM and Red Hat Commit $5 Billion to Secure Open Source Supply Chains Under “Project Lightwell”
item.description: Project Lightwell is designed to fix vulnerabilities without breaking what is already in production. The post IBM and Red Hat Commit $5 Billion to Secure Open Source Supply Chains Under “Project Lightwell” appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Qumulo NeuralProtect uses AI to detect and stop ransomware before encryption
item.description: Qumulo has unveiled Qumulo NeuralProtect, a ransomware resilience solution built to protect data at the storage layer by detecting and stopping threats before data is encrypted, corrupted, or lost. Integrated directly into the Qumulo Data Platform, NeuralProtect inspects every fi…

item.title: Qevlar’s new AI agents correlate CVEs, incident data, and active exploitation signals
item.description: Qevlar has announced a new set of AI agents designed to bridge the disconnect between Security Operations Centers (SOCs) and vulnerability management teams. The new capabilities help security teams correlate CVEs with live incident data for real-time risk prioritization, automati…

item.title: Microsoft Slams Public Zero-Day Disclosures Amid GitHub Researcher Account Removal
item.description: Microsoft has come out strongly in favor of Coordinated Vulnerability Disclosure (CVD), urging the research community to share their findings and give affected vendors an opportunity to better understand the impact and address them before they are publicly disclosed. The developm…

item.title: Indian CERT urges firms to contain exploited internet-facing flaws within 12 hours
item.description: India’s cybersecurity agency, CERT-In, has urged organizations to patch, mitigate, or isolate known exploited vulnerabilities affecting internet-facing “crown jewel” systems within 12 hours where feasible, warning that AI-assisted attacks are dramatically compressing the time bet…

item.title: Canadian man gets 33 years for using social media to coerce US children into sending sexual content
item.description: Prosecutors said the man spent years using fake online identities to contact children and manipulate them into sending sexually explicit images and videos.

item.title: DuckDuckGo sees 30% growth spike as Google forces AI on Search
item.description: DuckDuckGo says it experienced a significant spike in users following Google’s announcement of a sweeping AI-powered overhaul of Search at Google I/O 2026. According to figures shared by the privacy-focused search company, installs and visits increased sharply in the six days aft…

item.title: Zapier exploit chain shows how known anti-patterns compose into critical risk
item.description: A five-stage exploit chain disclosed by Token Security researchers turned a free Zapier account into write access on Zapier’s public developer SDK packages and on internal packages that load in every authenticated zapier.com session. Each link in the chain was a known anti-…

item.title: New Gogs zero-day flaw lets hackers get remote code execution
item.description: An unpatched zero-day vulnerability in the Gogs self-hosted Git service can allow attackers to gain remote code execution (RCE) on Internet-facing instances. [...]

item.title: IBM and Red Hat are betting $5 billion that open source needs a security guard
item.description: IBM and Red Hat announced Project Lightwell, a $5 billion commitment backed by new frontier AI capabilities and a global force of more than 20,000 engineers to help enterprises secure open source software. Together, these investments establish a new model for enterprise use of op…

item.title: State of Post Quantum Cryptography
item.description: Discussion of PQC relevant statistics that we see across our customers and other data sources.

item.title: IBM’s new $5B initiative will help enterprises rapidly patch open-source vulnerabilities
item.description: The tech giant’s project could make it easier for businesses to safely use open-source packages.

item.title: Attackers Move Past Typosquatting to Realistic Package Impersonation
item.description: Most malicious open source packages now mimic real code rather than rely on typosquatting

item.title: Threat Actors Exploit Critical FortiClient EMS Flaw to Deploy Credential Stealer
item.description: Threat actors are continuing to exploit a critical, now-patched security flaw impacting FortiClient Endpoint Management Server (EMS) deployments to deliver credential-stealing malware. "The campaign abused trusted endpoint management infrastructure to deliver malware across manag…

item.title: Sextortionist sentenced to 33 years for targeting 145 children
item.description: A Canadian man was sentenced to 33 years in prison after pleading guilty to targeting more than 145 children across the United States, some as young as 6 years old, in an eight-year-long sextortion scheme. [...]

item.title: Experts on Experts: Why Compliance is becoming Continuous
item.description: This week on Experts on Experts, I’m joined by Sergio Alonso – Rapid7’s Director of Trust, Risk, and Compliance – to talk about how compliance is changing and why many security teams are rethinking the way they approach readiness, reporting, and operational risk. One of the bigge…

item.title: New Cyber Command chief commissions MITRE to review modernization efforts.
item.description: CISA orders US agencies to patch maximum-severity cPanel flaw by tomorrow. Carnival confirms breach affecting just under six million people.

item.title: The CISO selling confidence in a market full of breach headlines
item.description: Engineering teams across enterprise IT are writing their own software with AI coding assistants, spinning up agents that act on their behalf, and assigning those agents the same access privileges their human creators hold. The shift has pulled the role of the chief information se…

item.title: Nordic CISOs Handle Rising Cyber Threats Remarkably Well
item.description: Artificial intelligence notwithstanding, the vast majority of CISOs in northern Europe say they're facing no more serious cyberattacks than they did two years ago.

item.title: XM Cyber enhances identity risk visibility with continuous exposure management capabilities
item.description: XM Cyber has announced platform enhancements aimed at helping organizations reduce identity risk, compounded by AI-enabled attackers. According to Gartner, “By 2028, 70% of CISOs will use identity visibility and intelligence capabilities to shrink the IAM attack surface, reducing…

item.title: Police arrest suspect in Ajax football club hack that exposed 300,000 fan records
item.description: The Dutch National Police arrested a man suspected of hacking into the computer systems of AFC Ajax, a football club from Amsterdam. “On the morning of Tuesday, May 26, detectives arrested a 35-year-old man from the municipality of Buren for computer intrusion at the Amsterdam fo…

item.title: Google Unveils AI Threat Defense Platform to Fight AI-Powered Cyberattacks
item.description: New AI Threat Defense platform combines capabilities from Mandiant, Wiz and Gemini to help customers fight AI with AI. The post Google Unveils AI Threat Defense Platform to Fight AI-Powered Cyberattacks appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Infosecurity Europe: Cybersecurity Staff Prefer CISOs With Real Attack Response Experience, Study Reveals
item.description: ISC2 survey of cybersecurity professionals suggests that staff want their information security leaders to have experienced reacting to a significant cyber incident

item.title: 2026 World Cup: Discussing The World’s Biggest Game’s Attack Surface
item.description: The 2026 World Cup presents major cyber risks from ransomware groups, state-aligned actors, and other groups targeting critical infrastructure. Learn more here. The post 2026 World Cup: Discussing The World’s Biggest Game’s Attack Surface appeared first on Unit 42 .

item.title: Carnival Cruise confirms data breach affecting nearly 6 million people
item.description: Carnival Corporation, the world's largest cruise line operator, has confirmed a data breach affecting nearly 6 million people claimed by the ShinyHunters extortion gang in April 2026. [...]

item.title: Carnival confirms data breach impacting nearly 6 million
item.description: Cruise giant Carnival has suffered yet another data breach, with ShinyHunters claiming to have stolen personal data affecting nearly 6 million people.

item.title: Romanian gets 5 years in prison for hacking Oregon govt network
item.description: A Romanian national was sentenced this week to 56 months in federal prison for breaking into an Oregon state government computer network and fr cyberattacks targeting dozens of other U.S. victims. [...]

item.title: Webinar: Why network incidents take too long to resolve
item.description: Many organizations can detect network issues quickly, but investigations and coordination often slow incident resolution. This webinar explores how automation and AI-assisted workflows can help IT teams reduce delays and improve response times. [...]

item.title: MyPillow listed on ransomware gang’s leak site, but denies it has been breached
item.description: A notorious ransomware gang claims to have stolen MyPillow's private data, but CEO Mike Lindell calls it a politically motivated "hit job." With the countdown ticking toward a massive dark web leak, who is telling the truth? Read more in my article on the Hot for Security blog.

item.title: New BTMOB Android Malware Enables Full Device Takeover
item.description: Delivered via phishing lures, the malware combines financial theft with data exfiltration and remote access. The post New BTMOB Android Malware Enables Full Device Takeover appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Cruise giant Carnival confirms data breach affecting nearly 6 million people
item.description: The company said the threat actor gained access to a limited portion of its IT environment last month after compromising an employee account. By the end of April, Carnival determined that the attacker had copied personal information from its systems.

item.title: Carnival begins notifying 6 million people of a data breach
item.description: Carnival Corporation has begun notifying roughly six million individuals that their personal information was stolen in the cyberattack claimed by the ShinyHunters extortion group earlier this year. The disclosure follows the public leak of data allegedly containing 8.7 million re…

item.title: Carnival Data Breach Exposed 6 Million People
item.description: Data breach leaves nearly 6 million Carnival customers navigating identity theft risks. The post Carnival Data Breach Exposed 6 Million People appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Cybercriminals sail away with data from 6 million Carnival customers
item.description: Carnival Corporation, one of the world’s largest cruise operators, confirmed a data breach weeks after the ShinyHunters hacking group claimed it had stolen millions of customer records. Carnival acknowledged a phishing incident involving a single employee account and stated that …

item.title: The Gentlemen ransomware: Dissecting a self-propagating Go encryptor
item.description: Microsoft Threat Intelligence presents a comprehensive analysis of The Gentlemen, a Go-based ransomware deployed by affiliates of Storm-2697 that combines per-file ephemeral key encryption with an aggressive self-propagation module to deploy itself across an entire network using …

item.title: ISC Stormcast For Thursday, May 28th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9948, (Thu, May 28th)
item.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

item.title: Nudge Security adds browser-based discovery for shadow AI agents
item.description: Nudge Security announced that its AI security platform offers discovery of shadow AI agents via the browser, extending its agent discovery capabilities to cover platforms that do not provide a public API for agent identity and inventory. The new browser-based agentic AI discovery…

item.title: Frontier AI models collapse under multi-turn AI attacks, Cisco finds
item.description: Attackers who probe large language models rarely give up after one refusal. They reframe, build context across turns, adopt personas, and escalate gradually. New research from Cisco’s AI threat intelligence team finds that the safety benchmarks used across the industry miss…

item.title: Checksum introduces Continuous Quality Agent for automated test generation and healing
item.description: Checksum has launched its Continuous Quality Agent, an autonomous system that runs nightly against deployed applications and automatically heals broken tests without waiting for an engineer to open a dashboard or write a prompt. AI coding has changed the constraint in software de…

item.title: JINX-0164 Targets Cryptocurrency Firms with Fake Recruiter Lures and macOS Malware
item.description: A new campaign orchestrated by a previously undocumented threat actor has targeted cryptocurrency organizations with an aim to facilitate digital asset theft using recruitment-themed social engineering and bespoke macOS malware. "These campaigns leveraged sophisticated social eng…

item.title: A single typo could derail your World Cup plans
item.description: Cybercriminals are spoofing Fédération Internationale de Football Association (FIFA) websites ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup, the FBI warns. The attackers are registering lookalike domains with small spelling changes or different domain endings to impersonate FIFA websites and …

item.title: New Threat Actor Jinx-0164 Targets Crypto Developers on macOS
item.description: New actor Jinx-0164 hit crypto developers with fake recruiter lures and macOS malware

item.title: New Edamame Platform Aims to Catch AI Coding Agents Going Off the Rails
item.description: France-based startup Edamame says its runtime verification platform uses host telemetry and AI analysis to detect coding-agent “intent drift,” secret theft and supply-chain attacks in real time. The post New Edamame Platform Aims to Catch AI Coding Agents Going Off the Rails appe…

item.title: Raising the Cybersecurity Stakes: Ante up for the Agentic Era
item.description: CISOs are now facing machine-speed attacks and asking, “How do I agent?” The industry must provide remediation at scale. The post Raising the Cybersecurity Stakes: Ante up for the Agentic Era appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: OpenAI prepares ChatGPT for the election misinformation wave
item.description: AI-generated election misinformation could shape public opinion and influence the lives of millions of people. To address those risks, OpenAI outlined a series of safeguards ahead of the 2026 election cycle. The company said its efforts will focus on helping users access voting i…

item.title: Digimarc adds provenance, audit, and verification controls for AI agent workflows
item.description: Digimarc has announced new provenance and verification infrastructure designed to secure autonomous and AI-enabled workflows. As enterprises increasingly adopt AI systems capable of generating content, orchestrating workflows, and taking action with minimal human intervention, es…
